title: On Realization of Back-Action-Evading Measurements and Quantum Non-Demolition Variables via Linear Systems Engineering
authors: Zhiyuan Dong, Weichao Liang, Guofeng Zhang
abstract: We establish a framework for realizing back-action-evading (BAE) measurements and quantum non-demolition (QND) variables in linear quantum systems. The key condition, a purely imaginary Hamiltonian with a real or imaginary coupling operator, enables BAE measurements of conjugate observables. Symmetric coupling further yields QND variables. For non-compliant systems, coherent feedback is designed to engineer BAE measurements. Crucially, the QND interaction condition simultaneously ensures BAE measurements and promotes the coupling operator to a QND observable.
summary: This paper develops a unified mathematical framework for achieving Back-Action-Evading measurements and QND variables in linear quantum systems. By using linear systems engineering, the authors identify the specific algebraic constraints on Hamiltonians and coupling operators needed to prevent noise from affecting measurement records. They demonstrate that even systems that do not naturally satisfy these conditions can be re-engineered using coherent feedback or direct coupling. The results are particularly applicable to quantum optomechanics and precision sensing.
main_problem: Establishing a systematic framework to realize Back-Action-Evading (BAE) measurements and Quantum Non-Demolition (QND) variables in linear quantum systems.
main_result: The authors derive algebraic conditions for BAE and QND realization, proving that a purely imaginary Hamiltonian with specific coupling properties enables BAE, and that coherent feedback or direct coupling can engineer these properties in non-compliant systems.
method: Utilizing linear systems engineering tools, including Kalman canonical forms, transfer function analysis, and coherent feedback control design.
model_or_system: The framework applies to linear quantum systems in continuous-mode measurement scenarios, described by Quantum Stochastic Differential Equations (QSDEs) and the Quantum Stochastic Master Equation (QSME).
key_observables: Conjugate quadratures (position and momentum), output quadratures, and the coupling operator.
important_parameters: Hamiltonian parameters (Omega), scattering matrix (S), coupling strength (lambda), and the distinction between bilateral and unilateral BAE regimes.
main_assumptions: The framework is restricted to linear quantum systems and assumes continuous-mode measurement.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows a coherent feedback network using a beamsplitter; Figure 2 illustrates a quantum optomechanical system platform.
paper_structure: The paper moves from defining BAE and QND conditions to establishing structural requirements, investigating SISO and MIMO regimes, and finally presenting synthesis methods via feedback and direct coupling.
why_interesting: It provides a rigorous control-theoretic approach to suppressing measurement back-action, which is essential for high-precision sensing and maintaining quantum coherence in open quantum systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
